.ddx/executions/20260424T064449-69011fdb/manifest.json:13:    "description": "## Observed

On the bead detail panel, a long bead id overflows and visually overlaps the Claim/Unclaim/Edit/Delete/Close buttons on the right. Reporter example: `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1` (~60 chars).

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadDetail.svelte:211-221` —

```svelte
<div class="flex shrink-0 items-center justify-between border-b ... px-6 py-4">
  <div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">
    <span class="shrink-0 font-mono text-xs ...">{bead.id}</span>       ← shrink-0 prevents truncation
    <span class="shrink-0 font-medium ...">{bead.status}</span>
    {#if bead.owner}
      <span class="truncate text-xs ...">@ {bead.owner}</span>
    {/if}
  </div>
  <div class="ml-3 flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2">
    [Claim/Unclaim] [Edit] [Delete] [Close]
  </div>
</div>
```

The bead-id span has `shrink-0`, so it never truncates regardless of length. The outer panel is `max-w-xl` (576px). A 60-char monospace id occupies roughly 400-500px on its own; combined with the status pill, owner chip, and the action-button group (also `shrink-0`), it pushes past the available width. Flex layout collapses by overlapping rather than wrapping, so the id draws on top of the buttons.

## Proposed direction

1. **Replace `shrink-0` on the bead-id span with `truncate min-w-0`.** The parent `<div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">` already establishes a shrinkable container.
2. **Add `title={bead.id}` so hovering shows the full value** (important — truncating monospace ids from the right loses the distinguishing suffix).
3. **Add a copy-to-clipboard icon button** next to the id so operators can grab the full value with one click when the id is truncated. Small icon button, no extra visual weight.
4. **Sanity check:** the reporter's example starts with `.` and has the shape of an execute-bead worktree name. Worth briefly checking whether something is producing such a value as a bead id (worktree name leaking into bead creation?) — but that's a separate investigation. The UI still needs to degrade gracefully regardless.

## Out of scope

- Changing bead id generation or enforcing max length on ids.
- Redesign of the detail-panel header layout.",
.ddx/executions/20260424T064449-69011fdb/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "**User story:** As a developer opening a bead with a long id, the id truncates cleanly without overlapping any action button, the full id is discoverable on hover and copyable with one click, and the header buttons remain fully clickable.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. The bead-id span in `BeadDetail.svelte` uses `truncate min-w-0` (or equivalent) instead of `shrink-0`. The parent flex container keeps `min-w-0`.
2. The span has `title={bead.id}` so hovering reveals the full value.
3. A copy-to-clipboard icon button sits immediately after the id. Clicking copies the full id to the clipboard and surfaces a subtle confirmation (existing toast / inline check mark — whichever matches project conventions).
4. Action buttons (Claim/Unclaim, Edit, Delete, Close) remain fully visible and clickable regardless of id length. Status pill and owner chip remain visible; they may wrap below the id on narrow viewports but never overlap the action group.
5. Playwright e2e:
   - Seeds a bead with a 60-char id (e.g. `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1`) and opens its detail panel.
   - Asserts the id element has a truncation class and that its rendered width is ≤ the parent container width minus the action-button group width.
   - Asserts the Delete button is clickable (`.click()` does not throw) and that its bounding rect does not intersect the id span's bounding rect.
   - Asserts hovering the id shows a tooltip (or reveals `title` attribute) with the full value, and clicking the copy button writes the full id to the clipboard.
6. Unit/visual: at `max-w-xl` width the header renders without horizontal scroll and without clipped action buttons for ids of length 8, 32, 60, and 120.",

- Redesign of the detail-panel header layout.
.ddx/executions/20260424T064449-69011fdb/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer opening a bead with a long id, the id truncates cleanly without overlapping any action button, the full id is discoverable on hover and copyable with one click, and the header buttons remain fully clickable.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. The bead-id span in `BeadDetail.svelte` uses `truncate min-w-0` (or equivalent) instead of `shrink-0`. The parent flex container keeps `min-w-0`.
2. The span has `title={bead.id}` so hovering reveals the full value.
3. A copy-to-clipboard icon button sits immediately after the id. Clicking copies the full id to the clipboard and surfaces a subtle confirmation (existing toast / inline check mark — whichever matches project conventions).
4. Action buttons (Claim/Unclaim, Edit, Delete, Close) remain fully visible and clickable regardless of id length. Status pill and owner chip remain visible; they may wrap below the id on narrow viewports but never overlap the action group.
5. Playwright e2e:
   - Seeds a bead with a 60-char id (e.g. `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1`) and opens its detail panel.
   - Asserts the id element has a truncation class and that its rendered width is ≤ the parent container width minus the action-button group width.
   - Asserts the Delete button is clickable (`.click()` does not throw) and that its bounding rect does not intersect the id span's bounding rect.
   - Asserts hovering the id shows a tooltip (or reveals `title` attribute) with the full value, and clicking the copy button writes the full id to the clipboard.
6. Unit/visual: at `max-w-xl` width the header renders without horizontal scroll and without clipped action buttons for ids of length 8, 32, 60, and 120.

.ddx/executions/20260424T031545-94fa4bb8/manifest.json:13:    "description": "## Observed

`git worktree list` on the current ddx repo shows **39 worktrees**:
- 1 main worktree (the clone itself).
- ~37 under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>/` from execute-bead runs. Branch names `worktree-agent-<hash>`.
- At least 1 under `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<beadId>-<ts>-<hash>` (detached HEAD) — different path convention, different semantics.

There is no UI visibility into any of this. A user cannot see which worktrees exist, what bead each one belongs to, whether each is active/abandoned/completed, or clean them up short of `git worktree prune` + `rm -rf`. The `.claude/worktrees/` entries also leak into other surfaces (already tracked):
- Documents view noise (`ddx-12cae4dd`).
- One was surviving in the project registry cleanup earlier today (`proj-4c1ca90e: agent-a7bf6d44`).

## Why the two path conventions?

- `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>` — created with a branch (`worktree-agent-<hash>`), likely by interactive execute-bead runs or an earlier worktree convention.
- `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<bead>-<ts>-<hash>` — detached HEAD, under `/tmp`, convention that ships with current execute-bead (this is what's producing the long "bead id"-shaped names that leaked into UI in `ddx-71fbe968`).

Two conventions is itself a smell: inconsistent path discipline makes cleanup harder and makes cross-referencing an execution bundle → worktree unreliable.

## What's needed

### Part 1 — Inventory resolver

- GraphQL: `executeBeadWorktrees(projectId)` → list of `{path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state, createdAt, lastActivityAt}`.
- Source: `git worktree list --porcelain` + filesystem stat + manifest lookup for executions whose `execution_dir` matches or whose path correlates.
- `state` ∈ `{active, completed, abandoned, orphan}`:
  - **active** — an execute-bead session is currently running against this worktree (cross-reference workers / sessions).
  - **completed** — associated execution bundle exists with a verdict; head matches `result_rev`.
  - **abandoned** — no recent activity (configurable, default 24h), no matching running worker, but worktree still present.
  - **orphan** — path on disk but `git worktree list` does not know about it (or vice versa).

### Part 2 — Sidebar + route

- New "Worktrees" view, or slot this into the Executions detail (`ddx-726bc5f2`) as a sidebar panel since every worktree is for some execution. Design call; recommend a dedicated view so orphans (which have no execution) are still visible.
- Route: `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/worktrees`.
- Columns: path, branch, bead (link), execution (link), state, created, last activity, disk usage.
- Row actions: **Open execution**, **Remove worktree** (confirm-gated; calls `git worktree remove` and cleans up `.claude/worktrees/<name>`), **Force remove** (for orphans; `-f`).

### Part 3 — Policy + cleanup

- Background sweep (server-side, already has a sweep pattern from project registry): every N minutes, list worktrees, detect abandoned state, emit a notice (but **never auto-delete** — user-confirmed cleanup only by default).
- `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run]` CLI command that enumerates and optionally removes abandoned worktrees. Same deletion path as the UI action.
- Config: `.ddx/config.yaml: worktrees.abandoned_after: 24h`, `worktrees.auto_prune: false`. Opt-in auto-prune for teams that want it.

### Part 4 — Path convention unification

- Decide a single canonical location for execute-bead worktrees. Recommend `.claude/worktrees/<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/` or a sibling `.ddx/worktrees/` — **pick one and stick with it**.
- Migrate existing paths lazily: on first sweep after upgrade, record the path as-is and leave it; new worktrees use the new convention. Old ones phase out as they complete or get cleaned.
- This is a separable sub-effort; acceptable to land Parts 1-3 against the current mixed reality and do Part 4 afterward.

## Out of scope

- Changing how execute-bead creates worktrees (beyond the canonical-location choice in Part 4).
- Documents-view noise — covered by `ddx-12cae4dd`.
- Long-id display overlap — covered by `ddx-71fbe968`.",

.ddx/executions/20260424T031545-94fa4bb8/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead: ddx-7aa810b8 — Track execute-bead worktree lifecycle

Verdict: no_changes. The bead is multi-surface and the AC set cannot be
completed to the specified quality in a single pass without committing
partial or red code across several surfaces. Per the bead instructions
("a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit"), this attempt
returns for breakdown/re-queue with a stronger budget or a pre-split bead set.

What the AC demands (11 items, summarized):

  1. GraphQL resolver executeBeadWorktrees(projectId) returning
     {path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state,
     createdAt, lastActivityAt} with unit tests for all four states.
  2. Pure, table-driven state classifier
     (active | completed | abandoned | orphan) with ≥2 edge cases per state.
  3. New SvelteKit route /nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/worktrees — sortable,
     filterable table.
  4. Row actions (Remove, Force-remove) wired through GraphQL mutations
     with confirm-gating and typed confirmation for force-remove.
  5. CLI: `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run] [--project] [--yes]`
     with an integration test against a fixture repo containing one of
     each state.
  6. Server-side sweep with configurable interval (default 15m), a
     GraphQL counter rollup type, and opt-in `worktrees.auto_prune` config.
  7. Cross-links to /executions/<id> (depends on ddx-726bc5f2) and a
     reciprocal "Worktree" section on the execution detail page.
  8. Fixture seed with ≥100 worktrees across states; p95 ≤ 200ms.
  9. Playwright e2e that seeds one of each state, asserts state badges,
     exercises Remove confirm flow, and asserts active-worktree gating.
 10. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ for Part 4 canonical path
     convention + migration plan (required only if Part 4 ships).
 11. Cross-reference notes to ddx-12cae4dd, ddx-71fbe968, ddx-726bc5f2,
     ddx-15f7ee0b.

What is in place already (checked):

  - Server package cli/internal/server/ with graphql resolver module,
    state.go / state_executions.go, workers.go, and a land_coordinator
    — the sweep / state-classification pattern has prior art here.
  - Frontend SvelteKit tree includes /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]
    with executions, workers, beads, documents, etc. No worktrees route yet.
  - Executions detail page already exists at .../executions/[executionId].
    Cross-link target in AC 7 is therefore implementable, not blocked on
    ddx-726bc5f2 (which may itself be partial or separate).
  - Two worktree path conventions confirmed at runtime:
      .claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>/   (branch-based, older)
      /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/ (detached)
    This execution itself is running from the /tmp convention.

What is blocking a single-pass completion:

  - The surface area spans Go (resolver + mutations + sweep + classifier +
    CLI subcommand + config plumbing), SvelteKit (new route, table with
    sort/filter, two confirm dialogs, mutation wiring, Houdini type
    regeneration), fixtures (≥100-worktree seeder), unit tests, an
    integration test, and a Playwright e2e. Each surface has its own
    test-green bar; shipping them all together green within one pass
    risks red commits on the surfaces that get squeezed last.
  - AC 5 (CLI subcommand) requires a new `ddx server state` command
    group; the current CLI surface exposes `ddx server` but does not
    have a `state` subcommand tree — adding one is a small but
    independent surface that interacts with the wider command layout.
  - AC 6 (sweep + counter + config) needs config-schema changes
    (.ddx/config.yaml worktrees block), which touches the config loader
    and every test that loads config. This is a cross-cutting change
    and deserves its own attempt slot.
  - AC 8's ≥100-worktree fixture is cheap to generate but the p95
    claim must be measured against a real handler — per the user's
    standing perf stance (feedback_testing_and_performance), a perf
    claim requires baseline + boundary + harness, not just a seed and
    a one-shot timing.

Recommended breakdown (for re-queue):

  A. Inventory resolver + pure classifier + unit tests.
     (AC 1, AC 2; pure, no UI, no config changes.)
  B. Worktrees route + list view + cross-links to executions.
     (AC 3, AC 7; depends on A; no mutations yet.)
  C. Remove / Force-remove mutations + row actions + Playwright e2e.
     (AC 4, AC 9; depends on B.)
  D. CLI worktree-prune subcommand + integration test.
     (AC 5; can run parallel to C once A is done.)
  E. Sweep + counter + config + opt-in auto_prune.
     (AC 6; depends on A; config-surface change.)
  F. ≥100-worktree fixture + perf harness with p95 measurement.
     (AC 8; depends on A; separate because of the perf methodology bar.)
  G. Path-convention design note + Part 4 migration plan.
     (AC 10, AC 11 for cross-refs; docs-only.)

A follow-up attempt should take A alone, or A+B together, to stay
within a single pass's green-bar budget.

## What's needed

### Part 1 — Inventory resolver

- GraphQL: `executeBeadWorktrees(projectId)` → list of `{path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state, createdAt, lastActivityAt}`.
- Source: `git worktree list --porcelain` + filesystem stat + manifest lookup for executions whose `execution_dir` matches or whose path correlates.
- `state` ∈ `{active, completed, abandoned, orphan}`:
  - **active** — an execute-bead session is currently running against this worktree (cross-reference workers / sessions).
  - **completed** — associated execution bundle exists with a verdict; head matches `result_rev`.
  - **abandoned** — no recent activity (configurable, default 24h), no matching running worker, but worktree still present.
  - **orphan** — path on disk but `git worktree list` does not know about it (or vice versa).

### Part 2 — Sidebar + route

- New "Worktrees" view, or slot this into the Executions detail (`ddx-726bc5f2`) as a sidebar panel since every worktree is for some execution. Design call; recommend a dedicated view so orphans (which have no execution) are still visible.
- Route: `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/worktrees`.
- Columns: path, branch, bead (link), execution (link), state, created, last activity, disk usage.
- Row actions: **Open execution**, **Remove worktree** (confirm-gated; calls `git worktree remove` and cleans up `.claude/worktrees/<name>`), **Force remove** (for orphans; `-f`).

### Part 3 — Policy + cleanup

- Background sweep (server-side, already has a sweep pattern from project registry): every N minutes, list worktrees, detect abandoned state, emit a notice (but **never auto-delete** — user-confirmed cleanup only by default).
- `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run]` CLI command that enumerates and optionally removes abandoned worktrees. Same deletion path as the UI action.
- Config: `.ddx/config.yaml: worktrees.abandoned_after: 24h`, `worktrees.auto_prune: false`. Opt-in auto-prune for teams that want it.

### Part 4 — Path convention unification

- Decide a single canonical location for execute-bead worktrees. Recommend `.claude/worktrees/<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/` or a sibling `.ddx/worktrees/` — **pick one and stick with it**.
- Migrate existing paths lazily: on first sweep after upgrade, record the path as-is and leave it; new worktrees use the new convention. Old ones phase out as they complete or get cleaned.
- This is a separable sub-effort; acceptable to land Parts 1-3 against the current mixed reality and do Part 4 afterward.

## Out of scope

- Changing how execute-bead creates worktrees (beyond the canonical-location choice in Part 4).
- Documents-view noise — covered by `ddx-12cae4dd`.
- Long-id display overlap — covered by `ddx-71fbe968`.",

.ddx/executions/20260423T055010-579b5503/prompt.md:5:## Observed

`git worktree list` on the current ddx repo shows **39 worktrees**:
- 1 main worktree (the clone itself).
- ~37 under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>/` from execute-bead runs. Branch names `worktree-agent-<hash>`.
- At least 1 under `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<beadId>-<ts>-<hash>` (detached HEAD) — different path convention, different semantics.

There is no UI visibility into any of this. A user cannot see which worktrees exist, what bead each one belongs to, whether each is active/abandoned/completed, or clean them up short of `git worktree prune` + `rm -rf`. The `.claude/worktrees/` entries also leak into other surfaces (already tracked):
- Documents view noise (`ddx-12cae4dd`).
- One was surviving in the project registry cleanup earlier today (`proj-4c1ca90e: agent-a7bf6d44`).

## Why the two path conventions?

- `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>` — created with a branch (`worktree-agent-<hash>`), likely by interactive execute-bead runs or an earlier worktree convention.
- `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<bead>-<ts>-<hash>` — detached HEAD, under `/tmp`, convention that ships with current execute-bead (this is what's producing the long "bead id"-shaped names that leaked into UI in `ddx-71fbe968`).

Two conventions is itself a smell: inconsistent path discipline makes cleanup harder and makes cross-referencing an execution bundle → worktree unreliable.

.ddx/executions/20260422T210219-768742df/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed

On the bead detail panel, a long bead id overflows and visually overlaps the Claim/Unclaim/Edit/Delete/Close buttons on the right. Reporter example: `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1` (~60 chars).

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadDetail.svelte:211-221` —

```svelte
<div class="flex shrink-0 items-center justify-between border-b ... px-6 py-4">
  <div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">
    <span class="shrink-0 font-mono text-xs ...">{bead.id}</span>       ← shrink-0 prevents truncation
    <span class="shrink-0 font-medium ...">{bead.status}</span>
    {#if bead.owner}
      <span class="truncate text-xs ...">@ {bead.owner}</span>
    {/if}
  </div>
  <div class="ml-3 flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2">
    [Claim/Unclaim] [Edit] [Delete] [Close]
  </div>
</div>
```

The bead-id span has `shrink-0`, so it never truncates regardless of length. The outer panel is `max-w-xl` (576px). A 60-char monospace id occupies roughly 400-500px on its own; combined with the status pill, owner chip, and the action-button group (also `shrink-0`), it pushes past the available width. Flex layout collapses by overlapping rather than wrapping, so the id draws on top of the buttons.

## Proposed direction

1. **Replace `shrink-0` on the bead-id span with `truncate min-w-0`.** The parent `<div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">` already establishes a shrinkable container.
2. **Add `title={bead.id}` so hovering shows the full value** (important — truncating monospace ids from the right loses the distinguishing suffix).
3. **Add a copy-to-clipboard icon button** next to the id so operators can grab the full value with one click when the id is truncated. Small icon button, no extra visual weight.
4. **Sanity check:** the reporter's example starts with `.` and has the shape of an execute-bead worktree name. Worth briefly checking whether something is producing such a value as a bead id (worktree name leaking into bead creation?) — but that's a separate investigation. The UI still needs to degrade gracefully regardless.

## Out of scope

- Changing bead id generation or enforcing max length on ids.
- Redesign of the detail-panel header layout.",
.ddx/executions/20260422T210219-768742df/manifest.json:15:    "acceptance": "**User story:** As a developer opening a bead with a long id, the id truncates cleanly without overlapping any action button, the full id is discoverable on hover and copyable with one click, and the header buttons remain fully clickable.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. The bead-id span in `BeadDetail.svelte` uses `truncate min-w-0` (or equivalent) instead of `shrink-0`. The parent flex container keeps `min-w-0`.
2. The span has `title={bead.id}` so hovering reveals the full value.
3. A copy-to-clipboard icon button sits immediately after the id. Clicking copies the full id to the clipboard and surfaces a subtle confirmation (existing toast / inline check mark — whichever matches project conventions).
4. Action buttons (Claim/Unclaim, Edit, Delete, Close) remain fully visible and clickable regardless of id length. Status pill and owner chip remain visible; they may wrap below the id on narrow viewports but never overlap the action group.
5. Playwright e2e:
   - Seeds a bead with a 60-char id (e.g. `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1`) and opens its detail panel.
   - Asserts the id element has a truncation class and that its rendered width is ≤ the parent container width minus the action-button group width.
   - Asserts the Delete button is clickable (`.click()` does not throw) and that its bounding rect does not intersect the id span's bounding rect.
   - Asserts hovering the id shows a tooltip (or reveals `title` attribute) with the full value, and clicking the copy button writes the full id to the clipboard.
6. Unit/visual: at `max-w-xl` width the header renders without horizontal scroll and without clipped action buttons for ids of length 8, 32, 60, and 120.",


The bead-id span has `shrink-0`, so it never truncates regardless of length. The outer panel is `max-w-xl` (576px). A 60-char monospace id occupies roughly 400-500px on its own; combined with the status pill, owner chip, and the action-button group (also `shrink-0`), it pushes past the available width. Flex layout collapses by overlapping rather than wrapping, so the id draws on top of the buttons.

## Proposed direction

1. **Replace `shrink-0` on the bead-id span with `truncate min-w-0`.** The parent `<div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">` already establishes a shrinkable container.
2. **Add `title={bead.id}` so hovering shows the full value** (important — truncating monospace ids from the right loses the distinguishing suffix).
3. **Add a copy-to-clipboard icon button** next to the id so operators can grab the full value with one click when the id is truncated. Small icon button, no extra visual weight.
4. **Sanity check:** the reporter's example starts with `.` and has the shape of an execute-bead worktree name. Worth briefly checking whether something is producing such a value as a bead id (worktree name leaking into bead creation?) — but that's a separate investigation. The UI still needs to degrade gracefully regardless.

## Out of scope

- Changing bead id generation or enforcing max length on ids.
- Redesign of the detail-panel header layout.

.ddx/executions/20260422T210221-5e050f23/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadDetail.svelte:211-221` —

```svelte
<div class="flex shrink-0 items-center justify-between border-b ... px-6 py-4">
  <div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">
    <span class="shrink-0 font-mono text-xs ...">{bead.id}</span>       ← shrink-0 prevents truncation
    <span class="shrink-0 font-medium ...">{bead.status}</span>
    {#if bead.owner}
      <span class="truncate text-xs ...">@ {bead.owner}</span>
    {/if}
  </div>
  <div class="ml-3 flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2">
    [Claim/Unclaim] [Edit] [Delete] [Close]
  </div>
</div>
```

The bead-id span has `shrink-0`, so it never truncates regardless of length. The outer panel is `max-w-xl` (576px). A 60-char monospace id occupies roughly 400-500px on its own; combined with the status pill, owner chip, and the action-button group (also `shrink-0`), it pushes past the available width. Flex layout collapses by overlapping rather than wrapping, so the id draws on top of the buttons.

## Proposed direction

1. **Replace `shrink-0` on the bead-id span with `truncate min-w-0`.** The parent `<div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">` already establishes a shrinkable container.
2. **Add `title={bead.id}` so hovering shows the full value** (important — truncating monospace ids from the right loses the distinguishing suffix).
3. **Add a copy-to-clipboard icon button** next to the id so operators can grab the full value with one click when the id is truncated. Small icon button, no extra visual weight.
4. **Sanity check:** the reporter's example starts with `.` and has the shape of an execute-bead worktree name. Worth briefly checking whether something is producing such a value as a bead id (worktree name leaking into bead creation?) — but that's a separate investigation. The UI still needs to degrade gracefully regardless.

## Out of scope

- Changing bead id generation or enforcing max length on ids.
- Redesign of the detail-panel header layout.",

1. The bead-id span in `BeadDetail.svelte` uses `truncate min-w-0` (or equivalent) instead of `shrink-0`. The parent flex container keeps `min-w-0`.
2. The span has `title={bead.id}` so hovering reveals the full value.
3. A copy-to-clipboard icon button sits immediately after the id. Clicking copies the full id to the clipboard and surfaces a subtle confirmation (existing toast / inline check mark — whichever matches project conventions).
4. Action buttons (Claim/Unclaim, Edit, Delete, Close) remain fully visible and clickable regardless of id length. Status pill and owner chip remain visible; they may wrap below the id on narrow viewports but never overlap the action group.
5. Playwright e2e:
   - Seeds a bead with a 60-char id (e.g. `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1`) and opens its detail panel.
   - Asserts the id element has a truncation class and that its rendered width is ≤ the parent container width minus the action-button group width.
   - Asserts the Delete button is clickable (`.click()` does not throw) and that its bounding rect does not intersect the id span's bounding rect.
   - Asserts hovering the id shows a tooltip (or reveals `title` attribute) with the full value, and clicking the copy button writes the full id to the clipboard.
6. Unit/visual: at `max-w-xl` width the header renders without horizontal scroll and without clipped action buttons for ids of length 8, 32, 60, and 120.",

.ddx/executions/20260422T234723-8b0a7b43/manifest.json:103:    "worktree": "tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-68c372a6-20260422T234723-8b0a7b43"
.ddx/executions/20260422T210135-7b3cb836/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed

`git worktree list` on the current ddx repo shows **39 worktrees**:
- 1 main worktree (the clone itself).
- ~37 under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>/` from execute-bead runs. Branch names `worktree-agent-<hash>`.
- At least 1 under `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<beadId>-<ts>-<hash>` (detached HEAD) — different path convention, different semantics.

There is no UI visibility into any of this. A user cannot see which worktrees exist, what bead each one belongs to, whether each is active/abandoned/completed, or clean them up short of `git worktree prune` + `rm -rf`. The `.claude/worktrees/` entries also leak into other surfaces (already tracked):
- Documents view noise (`ddx-12cae4dd`).
- One was surviving in the project registry cleanup earlier today (`proj-4c1ca90e: agent-a7bf6d44`).

## Why the two path conventions?

- `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>` — created with a branch (`worktree-agent-<hash>`), likely by interactive execute-bead runs or an earlier worktree convention.
- `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<bead>-<ts>-<hash>` — detached HEAD, under `/tmp`, convention that ships with current execute-bead (this is what's producing the long "bead id"-shaped names that leaked into UI in `ddx-71fbe968`).

Two conventions is itself a smell: inconsistent path discipline makes cleanup harder and makes cross-referencing an execution bundle → worktree unreliable.

## What's needed

### Part 1 — Inventory resolver

- GraphQL: `executeBeadWorktrees(projectId)` → list of `{path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state, createdAt, lastActivityAt}`.
- Source: `git worktree list --porcelain` + filesystem stat + manifest lookup for executions whose `execution_dir` matches or whose path correlates.
- `state` ∈ `{active, completed, abandoned, orphan}`:
  - **active** — an execute-bead session is currently running against this worktree (cross-reference workers / sessions).
  - **completed** — associated execution bundle exists with a verdict; head matches `result_rev`.
  - **abandoned** — no recent activity (configurable, default 24h), no matching running worker, but worktree still present.
  - **orphan** — path on disk but `git worktree list` does not know about it (or vice versa).

### Part 2 — Sidebar + route

- New "Worktrees" view, or slot this into the Executions detail (`ddx-726bc5f2`) as a sidebar panel since every worktree is for some execution. Design call; recommend a dedicated view so orphans (which have no execution) are still visible.
- Route: `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/worktrees`.
- Columns: path, branch, bead (link), execution (link), state, created, last activity, disk usage.
- Row actions: **Open execution**, **Remove worktree** (confirm-gated; calls `git worktree remove` and cleans up `.claude/worktrees/<name>`), **Force remove** (for orphans; `-f`).

### Part 3 — Policy + cleanup

- Background sweep (server-side, already has a sweep pattern from project registry): every N minutes, list worktrees, detect abandoned state, emit a notice (but **never auto-delete** — user-confirmed cleanup only by default).
- `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run]` CLI command that enumerates and optionally removes abandoned worktrees. Same deletion path as the UI action.
- Config: `.ddx/config.yaml: worktrees.abandoned_after: 24h`, `worktrees.auto_prune: false`. Opt-in auto-prune for teams that want it.

### Part 4 — Path convention unification

- Decide a single canonical location for execute-bead worktrees. Recommend `.claude/worktrees/<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/` or a sibling `.ddx/worktrees/` — **pick one and stick with it**.
- Migrate existing paths lazily: on first sweep after upgrade, record the path as-is and leave it; new worktrees use the new convention. Old ones phase out as they complete or get cleaned.
- This is a separable sub-effort; acceptable to land Parts 1-3 against the current mixed reality and do Part 4 afterward.

## Out of scope

- Changing how execute-bead creates worktrees (beyond the canonical-location choice in Part 4).
- Documents-view noise — covered by `ddx-12cae4dd`.
- Long-id display overlap — covered by `ddx-71fbe968`.",
.ddx/executions/20260422T210135-7b3cb836/manifest.json:56:    "worktree": "tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7aa810b8-20260422T210135-7b3cb836"
.ddx/executions/20260422T210224-bc942bcd/prompt.md:5:## Observed

On the bead detail panel, a long bead id overflows and visually overlaps the Claim/Unclaim/Edit/Delete/Close buttons on the right. Reporter example: `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1` (~60 chars).

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadDetail.svelte:211-221` —

```svelte
<div class="flex shrink-0 items-center justify-between border-b ... px-6 py-4">
  <div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">
    <span class="shrink-0 font-mono text-xs ...">{bead.id}</span>       ← shrink-0 prevents truncation
    <span class="shrink-0 font-medium ...">{bead.status}</span>
    {#if bead.owner}
      <span class="truncate text-xs ...">@ {bead.owner}</span>
    {/if}
  </div>
  <div class="ml-3 flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2">
    [Claim/Unclaim] [Edit] [Delete] [Close]
  </div>
</div>
```

The bead-id span has `shrink-0`, so it never truncates regardless of length. The outer panel is `max-w-xl` (576px). A 60-char monospace id occupies roughly 400-500px on its own; combined with the status pill, owner chip, and the action-button group (also `shrink-0`), it pushes past the available width. Flex layout collapses by overlapping rather than wrapping, so the id draws on top of the buttons.

## Proposed direction

1. **Replace `shrink-0` on the bead-id span with `truncate min-w-0`.** The parent `<div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">` already establishes a shrinkable container.
2. **Add `title={bead.id}` so hovering shows the full value** (important — truncating monospace ids from the right loses the distinguishing suffix).
3. **Add a copy-to-clipboard icon button** next to the id so operators can grab the full value with one click when the id is truncated. Small icon button, no extra visual weight.
4. **Sanity check:** the reporter's example starts with `.` and has the shape of an execute-bead worktree name. Worth briefly checking whether something is producing such a value as a bead id (worktree name leaking into bead creation?) — but that's a separate investigation. The UI still needs to degrade gracefully regardless.

## Out of scope

- Changing bead id generation or enforcing max length on ids.
- Redesign of the detail-panel header layout.

4. Action buttons (Claim/Unclaim, Edit, Delete, Close) remain fully visible and clickable regardless of id length. Status pill and owner chip remain visible; they may wrap below the id on narrow viewports but never overlap the action group.
5. Playwright e2e:
   - Seeds a bead with a 60-char id (e.g. `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-526efaf1`) and opens its detail panel.
   - Asserts the id element has a truncation class and that its rendered width is ≤ the parent container width minus the action-button group width.
   - Asserts the Delete button is clickable (`.click()` does not throw) and that its bounding rect does not intersect the id span's bounding rect.
   - Asserts hovering the id shows a tooltip (or reveals `title` attribute) with the full value, and clicking the copy button writes the full id to the clipboard.
6. Unit/visual: at `max-w-xl` width the header renders without horizontal scroll and without clipped action buttons for ids of length 8, 32, 60, and 120.

  - **abandoned** — no recent activity (configurable, default 24h), no matching running worker, but worktree still present.
  - **orphan** — path on disk but `git worktree list` does not know about it (or vice versa).

### Part 2 — Sidebar + route

- New "Worktrees" view, or slot this into the Executions detail (`ddx-726bc5f2`) as a sidebar panel since every worktree is for some execution. Design call; recommend a dedicated view so orphans (which have no execution) are still visible.
- Route: `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/worktrees`.
- Columns: path, branch, bead (link), execution (link), state, created, last activity, disk usage.
- Row actions: **Open execution**, **Remove worktree** (confirm-gated; calls `git worktree remove` and cleans up `.claude/worktrees/<name>`), **Force remove** (for orphans; `-f`).

### Part 3 — Policy + cleanup

- Background sweep (server-side, already has a sweep pattern from project registry): every N minutes, list worktrees, detect abandoned state, emit a notice (but **never auto-delete** — user-confirmed cleanup only by default).
- `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run]` CLI command that enumerates and optionally removes abandoned worktrees. Same deletion path as the UI action.
- Config: `.ddx/config.yaml: worktrees.abandoned_after: 24h`, `worktrees.auto_prune: false`. Opt-in auto-prune for teams that want it.

### Part 4 — Path convention unification

- Decide a single canonical location for execute-bead worktrees. Recommend `.claude/worktrees/<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/` or a sibling `.ddx/worktrees/` — **pick one and stick with it**.
- Migrate existing paths lazily: on first sweep after upgrade, record the path as-is and leave it; new worktrees use the new convention. Old ones phase out as they complete or get cleaned.
- This is a separable sub-effort; acceptable to land Parts 1-3 against the current mixed reality and do Part 4 afterward.

## Out of scope

- Changing how execute-bead creates worktrees (beyond the canonical-location choice in Part 4).
- Documents-view noise — covered by `ddx-12cae4dd`.
- Long-id display overlap — covered by `ddx-71fbe968`.",

.ddx/executions/20260422T210132-b1e8d21a/prompt.md:5:## Observed

`git worktree list` on the current ddx repo shows **39 worktrees**:
- 1 main worktree (the clone itself).
- ~37 under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>/` from execute-bead runs. Branch names `worktree-agent-<hash>`.
- At least 1 under `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<beadId>-<ts>-<hash>` (detached HEAD) — different path convention, different semantics.

There is no UI visibility into any of this. A user cannot see which worktrees exist, what bead each one belongs to, whether each is active/abandoned/completed, or clean them up short of `git worktree prune` + `rm -rf`. The `.claude/worktrees/` entries also leak into other surfaces (already tracked):
- Documents view noise (`ddx-12cae4dd`).
- One was surviving in the project registry cleanup earlier today (`proj-4c1ca90e: agent-a7bf6d44`).

## Why the two path conventions?

- `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>` — created with a branch (`worktree-agent-<hash>`), likely by interactive execute-bead runs or an earlier worktree convention.
- `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-<bead>-<ts>-<hash>` — detached HEAD, under `/tmp`, convention that ships with current execute-bead (this is what's producing the long "bead id"-shaped names that leaked into UI in `ddx-71fbe968`).

Two conventions is itself a smell: inconsistent path discipline makes cleanup harder and makes cross-referencing an execution bundle → worktree unreliable.

## What's needed

### Part 1 — Inventory resolver

- GraphQL: `executeBeadWorktrees(projectId)` → list of `{path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state, createdAt, lastActivityAt}`.
- Source: `git worktree list --porcelain` + filesystem stat + manifest lookup for executions whose `execution_dir` matches or whose path correlates.
- `state` ∈ `{active, completed, abandoned, orphan}`:
  - **active** — an execute-bead session is currently running against this worktree (cross-reference workers / sessions).
  - **completed** — associated execution bundle exists with a verdict; head matches `result_rev`.
  - **abandoned** — no recent activity (configurable, default 24h), no matching running worker, but worktree still present.
  - **orphan** — path on disk but `git worktree list` does not know about it (or vice versa).

- Row actions: **Open execution**, **Remove worktree** (confirm-gated; calls `git worktree remove` and cleans up `.claude/worktrees/<name>`), **Force remove** (for orphans; `-f`).

### Part 3 — Policy + cleanup

- Background sweep (server-side, already has a sweep pattern from project registry): every N minutes, list worktrees, detect abandoned state, emit a notice (but **never auto-delete** — user-confirmed cleanup only by default).
- `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run]` CLI command that enumerates and optionally removes abandoned worktrees. Same deletion path as the UI action.
- Config: `.ddx/config.yaml: worktrees.abandoned_after: 24h`, `worktrees.auto_prune: false`. Opt-in auto-prune for teams that want it.

### Part 4 — Path convention unification

- Decide a single canonical location for execute-bead worktrees. Recommend `.claude/worktrees/<bead>-<ts>-<hash>/` or a sibling `.ddx/worktrees/` — **pick one and stick with it**.
- Migrate existing paths lazily: on first sweep after upgrade, record the path as-is and leave it; new worktrees use the new convention. Old ones phase out as they complete or get cleaned.

.ddx/executions/20260415T011331-36e59382/manifest.json:14:    "description": "The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.

## Observed

Running 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):

1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.

2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:

    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    ...

3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.

4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.

## Expected

- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate
- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely
- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin

## Scope

- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status
- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn
- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls
- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget

## Evidence

- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)
- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master
- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally
- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic
- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome
- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)",

.ddx/executions/20260418T064927-78e29296/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead ddx-4817edfd was a tracker-only bead: its AC was to file four sub-beads
and close the parent as 'decomposed'. All five AC items are satisfied via
`ddx bead create` and `ddx bead update --status closed`, but those mutations
land in the main repo's bead store at /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl
(outside this worktree's git tree), so `git status` in this worktree shows no
intentional changes to commit. Hence this rationale instead of an empty/no-op
commit.

(a) What is done

1. Four sub-beads filed with parent=ddx-4817edfd:

   - .execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-f5cec42a (P2)
     "routing: context-window gating in evaluateCandidate"
     Smallest useful slice: RouteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens, reject
     candidates whose known context window is below it; unknown passes with
     warning. Caller-side estimate via char/4 in execute-bead. Files:
     cli/internal/agent/{types,routing,catalog}.go and the bead-prompt
     assembly site.

   - .execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-854cb8a7 (P2)
     "routing: tool-calling support gating with per-flavor capability metadata"
     SupportsTools tri-state on ModelEntry/Surface, curated capability table
     in a new cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go, discovery enrichment via
     /v1/models/status, RouteRequest.RequiresTools (execute-bead sets true),
     rejection in evaluateCandidate.

   - .execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-bb230ad5 (P3)
     "routing: effort/permissions gating per provider+model (not just per harness)"
     SupportsEffort and SupportsPermission on the curated table; reject when
     RouteRequest names a value not honored by the (provider, model) combo.
     Documents the openrouter-non-reasoning-model exception inline.

   - .execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-22cadb21 (P4)
     "routing: structured-output (JSON-mode) gating — likely incidental to tool-calling"
     Decision-gate-first bead: verify the SupportsTools => SupportsStructuredOutput
     hypothesis on currently-routed providers; if true, close as 'covered by
     tool-calling gating'; otherwise add SupportsStructuredOutput parallel
     to the tool-calling bead.

2. Parent ddx-4817edfd updated to status=closed with notes referencing each
   sub-bead by ID and reaffirming that the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641 does
   NOT depend on these for its exit gate (Phase 2 work, tracked separately).

AC walk-through:
  AC1: context-window sub-bead exists and is prioritized -> ddx-...-f5cec42a (P2). PASS
  AC2: tool-calling sub-bead exists -> ddx-...-854cb8a7 (P2). PASS
  AC3: effort/permissions sub-bead exists -> ddx-...-bb230ad5 (P3). PASS
  AC4: structured-output sub-bead exists -> ddx-...-22cadb21 (P4). PASS
  AC5: this bead closed as 'decomposed - see sub-beads' -> ddx-4817edfd status=closed
       with notes pointing at the four sub-bead IDs; no dependency edge added
       to ddx-2d974641 from any of them. PASS

(b) What is blocking

Nothing functional. One cosmetic snag worth noting for a separate ddx-side
bead: `ddx bead create` invoked from inside this execution worktree generates
IDs whose prefix is the worktree directory's basename
(.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-XXXX) instead of the
conventional 'ddx-XXXX' namespace. The beads themselves are correct (right
parent, right fields, queryable by `ddx bead show <full-id>`); only the IDs
are ugly. This is an upstream wart in DDx's bead-ID generation that probably
derives the prefix from cwd basename rather than from the configured
namespace; it should be filed as a separate ddx-CLI bead, not patched as
part of this work.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need

None for THIS bead — the AC is met. The four sub-beads themselves are
follow-up work owned by future execute-bead runs (or hand work) and will be
queued via `ddx bead ready` when the autorouting epic moves into Phase 2.
The execute-bead orchestrator can mark this bead complete based on the
status=closed transition recorded in the main repo's .ddx/beads.jsonl.",

  <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` → 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles
- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)
- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order
- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)
- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep
- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)
- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths
- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)
- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly
- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean

  <instructions>
You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above — it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract.

## How to work

1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding.

2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one.

3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code.

4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work.

5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress".

## Constraints

- Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above.
- Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation.
- Do not run `ddx init`.
- Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- Commit with `git add <specific files> && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` — there may be other WIP in the worktree.

## Done definition

`cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done.

SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Adds tables for:\n4\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\n5\t//! - users: global user identities\n6\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\n7\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\n8\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\n9\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\n12\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\n13\t\n14\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\n15\t///\n16\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\n17\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\n18\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t    // Create tenants table.\n20\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t    conn.execute_batch(\n22\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t        )\",\n29\t    )\n30\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n31\t\n32\t    // Create users table.\n33\t    conn.execute_batch(\n34\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n35\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n36\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t            email              TEXT,\n38\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\n40\t        )\",\n41\t    )\n42\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t    // Create user_identities table.\n45\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t    conn.execute_batch(\n47\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n50\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n51\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n52\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n53\t        )\",\n54\t    )\n55\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Create tenant_users table.\n58\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\n59\t    conn.execute_batch(\n60\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\n61\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n62\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n63\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\n64\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n65\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\n66\t        )\",\n67\t    )\n68\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_users table: {e}\"))?;\n69\t\n70\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\n71\t    conn.execute_batch(\n72\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\n73\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n74\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\n75\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n76\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, database_name)\n77\t        )\",\n78\t    )\n79\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_databases table: {e}\"))?;\n80\t\n81\t    // Create credential_revocations table.\n82\t    conn.execute_batch(\n83\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS credential_revocations (\n84\t            jti            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n85\t            revoked_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n86\t            revoked_by     TEXT\n87\t        )\",\n88\t    )\n89\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"credential_revocations table: {e}\"))?;\n90\t\n91\t    Ok(())\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DGpwMtL9EAnaRPBK3GqEBs","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n36\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             └─ route handler\n38\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  └─ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}
